nodes.label: Team Roster Fallback
nodes.rationale: The workflow reads .squad/team.md first and falls back to .ai-team/team.md so both roster layouts can drive assignments.

nodes.label: Forbidden Path Stripping
nodes.rationale: Squad state and proposal paths are removed from the preview merge so internal coordination files are not shipped in a release.

nodes.label: Idempotent Tag Creation
nodes.rationale: The workflow checks for an existing version tag and skips tag and release creation when it already exists.

nodes.label: Lead Fallback Routing
nodes.rationale: Issues without a capability or domain match are assigned to the Lead for further analysis.

nodes.label: Live Published Post Index
nodes.rationale: Published state is derived from content/posts on every run so repository reality wins over stale ledger data and the system self-repairs after renames or deletions.

nodes.label: Underscore Template Convention
nodes.rationale: Topic files beginning with an underscore are treated as examples and ignored by the scanner.

nodes.label: Server-Side Domain Allowlist
nodes.rationale: The allowlist is controlled by the orchestrator rather than model output to constrain discovery and image retrieval to approved domains.

nodes.label: Fail-Closed Publication Date Validation
nodes.rationale: Only Tavily publication dates or structural URL dates are trusted; undated primary material cannot qualify a candidate as fresh.

nodes.label: Untrusted Source Content Handling
nodes.rationale: External content is sanitized and delimited as untrusted data so embedded instructions cannot control prompts, filesystem writes, or citations.

nodes.label: Source Image Download Security
nodes.rationale: Source images are constrained by allowlisted hosts, redirect revalidation, exact candidate URLs, magic-number checks, and a byte limit to mitigate SSRF and invalid downloads.

nodes.label: Safe YAML Serialization
nodes.rationale: yaml.safe_dump serializes untrusted model output without concatenating it into YAML and preserves escaping and Unicode.

nodes.label: Tool Mastery Before Upgrades
nodes.rationale: Better tools do not guarantee better work; practitioners should exhaust existing capabilities through skill before requesting replacements.

nodes.label: Frozen Requirements
nodes.rationale: Application development is easy only when functional requirements remain frozen, paralleling walking over frozen water.

nodes.label: SYS Schema Isolation
nodes.rationale: A narrow proxy package exposes file-listing results while preventing application schemas from receiving general access to SYS internals.

nodes.label: SearchFiles Memory Risk
nodes.rationale: Recursive searches with many matching files can consume substantial memory because X$KRBMSFT is an in-memory table.

nodes.label: Requirements Control
nodes.rationale: System requirements are compared to balloons that remain controlled only while held firmly.

nodes.label: Publish-Time Minification
nodes.rationale: Minification runs on packaged Release output so downloaded assets shrink without changing the original development source files.

nodes.label: UsingTask Over Import
nodes.rationale: UsingTask points to source-controlled DLLs so every contributor uses the same Ajax Minifier version and location.
